headline: Bills Gear Up as Stadium Buzz Grows
writeup: The Buffalo Bills are generating significant buzz heading into training camp, with the new Highmark Stadium already sparking both excitement and controversy after open practice announcements drew mixed fan reactions. Josh Allen continues to be the anchor of the franchise, named the team's 'biggest strength' for 2026 as the Bills earned a No. 4 spot in ESPN's roster rankings — though the offense's supporting cast ranked near the bottom of the league in playmaker grades. Defensive coordinator Jim Leonhard faces scrutiny over late-down struggles, while analysts have flagged a major defensive weakness that could haunt Buffalo in 2026. On the feel-good side, new receiver DJ Moore won over fans by buying up his own merchandise at a local mall and giving it away for free, and running back James Cook hosted a youth football camp — even as the NFL continues to draw criticism for perceived disrespect toward the star back. With training camp on the horizon, roster battles are heating up and the Bills' path to a Super Bowl run will depend on whether their depth players can step up alongside their superstar quarterback.
